output.summary: Applied CSS fix to keep inner blocks (discourse div) in the right column (column 2) of the grid even when ThemeHeading is hidden. Added `md:col-start-2` class to the discourse wrapper in both TSX and PHP templates.

changes: Added `md:col-start-2` class to discourse div wrapper (line 89)
This ensures the inner blocks always appear in the second column of the grid at md breakpoint and above, regardless of whether ThemeHeading is rendered
Added `md:col-start-2` class to discourse div wrapper (line 44)
This ensures the inner blocks always appear in the second column of the grid at md breakpoint and above, regardless of whether ThemeHeading is rendered

testPage.note: Test page already contains the 'No ThemeHeading Content' variation (variation #3) which tests this scenario. No update needed.

fixesApplied.description: Inner blocks collapse to left column when ThemeHeading is hidden
fixesApplied.resolution: Added `md:col-start-2` class to the discourse div wrapper in both TSX and PHP. This CSS grid placement property forces the inner blocks to always occupy the second column (right side) of the two-column grid at md breakpoint and above, regardless of whether the ThemeHeading (left column) is rendered. On mobile (below md breakpoint), the grid is single column so the placement has no effect.

technicalDetails.reasoning: The `md:col-start-2` class explicitly positions the discourse div to start at grid column 2 (the right column) at the md breakpoint and above. This is a CSS-only solution that works regardless of whether the ThemeHeading is rendered. When ThemeHeading is hidden, the first column remains empty and the discourse div appears in the second column as intended. On mobile (below md breakpoint), the grid is `grid-cols-1` so the column start has no effect and content flows normally.
technicalDetails.alternative: Could have used conditional classes based on hasContent, but CSS grid placement is simpler and more maintainable.

notes: This fix ensures the 'No ThemeHeading Content' test variation displays correctly with inner blocks on the right side.
The fix is responsive-aware: only applies at md breakpoint and above where the two-column grid is active.
TSX and PHP remain perfectly in sync with identical class application.
Build and validation pass with no errors.
